bids.reasoning: At t=0.00 there is no time pressure to concede much, so I am targeting a near-maximum utility bid for myself. The opponent’s opening offer gives me only moderate utility and their message is an emotional appeal rather than evidence of constraints, so I will counter with my strongest valid package.

I targeted a very high-utility bid for us, conceding only on the lowest-weight issueD, for an estimated utility of about 0.961. Since it is still very early at t=0.02 and the opponent’s second offer actually reduced my utility rather than improving it, there is no reason to make major concessions yet. Their messages are emotional appeals, but the actual offer history suggests they have not meaningfully moved toward my priorities.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.93 because it is still very early in the negotiation and the opponent’s offers have actually decreased in utility to me rather than improving. Their messages are cooperative but unsupported by concessions, so I will make only a small move on my lowest-weight issue while holding firm on the three high-importance issues.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid around 0.96 because it is still very early at t=0.06 and the opponent’s offers have not materially improved for me, staying around low utility. Their messages are cooperative/emotional but not backed by meaningful concessions, so I will only concede on the lowest-weight issueD while holding firm on the three important issues.

At t=0.08 it is still very early, and the opponent’s offers have not shown a clear concession pattern in terms of my utility, fluctuating around relatively low values. I am therefore holding firm on the high-weight issues A, B, and C, while making only a small concession on the low-weight issueD and avoiding an exact repeat of my immediately previous bid.

At t=0.10 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid for me while making a small concession on the low-weight issueD. The opponent’s offers remain low for my utility and do not show a consistent concession trend, though they repeatedly choose issueD:valueC, so I can accommodate that without giving up much. Their messages are cooperative but mostly emotional appeals, so I rely on the offer pattern rather than the wording.

I am still targeting a very high-utility bid because it is early in the negotiation (t=0.12) and the opponent’s offers have not shown meaningful concession toward my high-weight priorities. At t=0.14 it is still early, so I am targeting a very high-utility bid for myself while making a low-cost concession on issueD, which the opponent repeatedly selects as valueC. I targeted a high-utility bid around 0.92, conceding only on the lowest-weight issueD while holding firm on the three 0.3-weight issues. At t=0.16 it is still early, and the opponent’s offers remain low-value to me without a clear concession trend, so their cooperative messages do not justify larger concessions yet.
